new_classes.definition_en: A whisky produced by blending two or more single malt whiskies that have been distilled at different distilleries, classified under CN subheadings 2208 30 41 and 2208 30 49 (Chapter 22).
A quality characterising a whisky that has been produced by blending spirit from two or more distinct distilleries, as opposed to a single-distillery origin.

new_classes.label_en: Blended Malt Scotch Whisky in Containers of 2 Litres or Less (CN 22083041)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Malt Scotch Whisky in Behältnissen bis 2 Liter (KN 22083041)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended malt Scotch whisky presented in containers holding 2 litres or less, classified under CN subheading 2208 30 41 (Chapter 22).

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three key distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) blended malt whisky is produced from two or more single malt whiskies from different distilleries (modelled via MultipleDistilleryOrigin quality and complement of SingleDistilleryOrigin); (2) the malt/barley basis (via MaltedBarleyMashBasis); (3) the container size threshold of ≤ 2 L for CN 22083041 (via maxContainerVolumeL decimalRange). The Scotch origin criterion (Scotland) is not fully formalised as no geographic origin quality for Scotland exists in the TBox yet; this accounts for the 0.15 coverage gap. The sibling code 22083049 (> 2 L containers) is referenced in the legal note but is a separate CN code outside this node's scope.
